nonBlockingIssues.description: Stale line number reference in main.rs AUDIT header (says 'DSB at line 132' but DSB is now at line 148 after header insertion). Documentation inaccuracy only.
Missing // AUDIT: comments on memory.rs, paging.rs, page_allocator.rs. AGENTS.md convention requires these markers.
Missing // AUDIT: comments on heap.rs, slab.rs, memory_region.rs, memory_mapped_io.rs. Same convention gap.
align_up/align_up_u64 wrapping fix silently returns 0 on overflow. Consider debug_assert or Option return for defense-in-depth.
Happy-path MMIO tests bypass the mmio_read32/mmio_write32 stubs and call core::ptr directly, not exercising the debug_assert guards on aligned addresses.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add explicit reminder in kernel-auditor skill (Step 3 or Step 8) to add // AUDIT: marker comments to each reviewed file. Currently this convention is only in AGENTS.md and 2 of 3 workers missed it.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Features memory-paging-pagealloc-audit and allocator-mmio-audit both omitted AUDIT markers on all 7 reviewed files, while assembly-boot-audit (different worker) included them on all 4 files. The skill procedure mentions // SAFETY: comments but not // AUDIT: markers.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify in kernel-auditor skill Step 5 that TDD failing-first is not applicable for documentation-only changes (SAFETY comments) or defense-in-depth additions (debug_assert). The no-bugs-found path should be more prominent.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Workers for allocator-mmio-audit and assembly-boot-audit both flagged in skillFeedback that TDD discipline doesn't map cleanly to documentation-only audit work.

rejectedObservations.observation: AGENTS.md convention for // AUDIT: comments needs 'stronger enforcement (blocking review criteria)'
rejectedObservations.reason: The convention serves as an in-code record of review. Making it blocking would be disproportionate — the commit messages already serve as audit records. Suggesting skill improvement instead.
